raw_consumer_statement: I ordered a pair of shoes from a third-party seller called XXXX, but they turned out to be fake. Despite multiple requests for a shipping label, they ignored me, forcing me to contact Amex for help. After about a week, they finally provided a label, but I was out of town at the time. Once I returned, I promptly sent the shoes back, and the merchant approved the return. However, I still haven't received a refund or credit, leaving me with a bill to Amex. They refuse to waive the charge due to the merchant 's three-day return policy, even though I repeatedly asked for a shipping label to return the counterfeit shoes. It feels unfair that I'm stuck with this bill when the merchant is unresponsive and I was the victim of their mistake.
